_note: The Emperors of Ancient China — a SEPARATE East Asian tradition, deliberately NOT connected to Adam's line. Two honest reasons: (1) no documented bloodline links China's legendary rulers to anyone else in this tree — it is a distinct mythological/dynastic tradition, and (2) it is a king-list stitched together from myth (the Three Sovereigns), legend (the Five Emperors), and semi-history (the Xia dynasty), not a single verified father-son bloodline. Rendered as a SUCCESSION chain (a king-list), not a strict bloodline — the header says so — though real father-son links are noted where tradition records them (e.g. most of the Xia kings). Status is flagged rigorously: MYTHICAL (Three Sovereigns: Fuxi, Nuwa, Shennong), LEGENDARY (Five Emperors: Huangdi/Yellow Emperor, Zhuanxu, Ku, Yao, Shun), SEMI-HISTORICAL (the Xia dynasty, Yu the Great down to Jie — traditional but not yet archaeologically confirmed). Dates follow the traditional/received chronology (Sima Qian's Shiji, the Xia-Shang-Zhou Chronology Project's c.2070-1600 BC for the Xia). Note the flood parallel: Yu the Great earns the Mandate by taming the great flood — a flood-hero motif echoing Noah and Ziusudra. All ids prefixed CN_ so they classify to the CHINA house. This is its own root; a separate island by design.

a.label:
The Emperors of Ancient China
c.2900 BC · legendary rulers & the Xia dynasty (a king-list, not a strict bloodline)
· a separate tradition with no link to Adam's line

children.label:
Fuxi (Fu Hsi)
c.2852 BC · first of the Three Sovereigns; gave humanity fishing, hunting & the eight trigrams · MYTHICAL

Nuwa (Nü-wa)
c.2850 BC · consort & sister of Fuxi; mother-goddess who mended the sky & shaped humankind from clay · MYTHICAL

Shennong (the Yan Emperor)
c.2737 BC · 'Divine Farmer'; taught agriculture & herbal medicine · MYTHICAL

Yellow Emperor (Huangdi)
c.2697 BC · first of the Five Emperors; legendary ancestor of the Chinese people · LEGENDARY

Zhuanxu (Gaoyang)
c.2514 BC · grandson of the Yellow Emperor; reformer of religion & the calendar · LEGENDARY

Emperor Ku (Gaoxin)
c.2436 BC · great-grandson of the Yellow Emperor; wise & impartial ruler · LEGENDARY

Emperor Yao
c.2356 BC · model sage-king; passed the throne by merit, not blood, to Shun · LEGENDARY

Emperor Shun
c.2255 BC · last of the Five Emperors; chosen for virtue; ceded the throne to Yu · LEGENDARY

Yu the Great
c.2070 BC · founder of the Xia dynasty; tamed the great flood & earned the Mandate — the Chinese flood-hero, echoing Noah & Ziusudra · SEMI-HISTORICAL

Qi of Xia
c.2025 BC · son of Yu; broke the abdication custom to found hereditary dynastic rule · SEMI-HISTORICAL

Tai Kang
c.2015 BC · son of Qi; lost control of the state (the 'Five Sons' exile) · SEMI-HISTORICAL

Zhong Kang
c.1985 BC · brother of Tai Kang; ruled amid the loss of Xia power · SEMI-HISTORICAL

Xiang
c.1970 BC · son of Zhong Kang; killed as the usurper Han Zhuo seized the throne · SEMI-HISTORICAL

Shao Kang
c.1940 BC · posthumous son of Xiang; the 'Shao Kang Restoration' won back the Xia throne · SEMI-HISTORICAL

Zhu (Yu Shu)
c.1915 BC · son of Shao Kang; consolidated the restored dynasty & expanded eastward · SEMI-HISTORICAL

Kong Jia
c.1740 BC · later Xia king whose misrule & superstition began the dynasty's decline · SEMI-HISTORICAL

Jie of Xia
c.1650 BC · last Xia king; tyrant overthrown by Tang, ending the dynasty & opening the Shang · SEMI-HISTORICAL
